meta.name: Caribbean Curry Goat
meta.subtitle: Jamaican-style curry goat with aromatics, thyme, Scotch bonnet, and rich gravy

source.name: ssa.cuisines.caribbean
notes: Canonical SSA Caribbean baseline curry goat.
Designed for deterministic planning + adaptation (stovetop braise; pressure cooker variant included).

yield.notes: Serves 6 with rice; 4–5 if served as a hearty stew. Recipe scales well for batch cooking.

required.label: Heavy pot or Dutch oven
required.notes: A tight-fitting lid helps the goat braise tender.

optional.label: Mortar & pestle
optional.notes: Nice for crushing allspice/pepper and blooming curry.

ingredients.label: Goat meat, bone-in stew cut
notes: Shoulder/leg cuts are ideal. Bone-in gives richer gravy.
Trim only excess surface fat; some fat is good for braising.

ingredients.label: Lime juice
notes: Used for cleaning/seasoning goat. Vinegar can substitute.
If you prefer less tang, reduce to 1 tbsp.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
notes: Adjust to taste; broth and curry blends vary in saltiness.

ingredients.label: Allspice (ground) or crushed pimento berries
notes: If using whole pimento, crush 6–8 berries.

ingredients.label: Fresh thyme
notes: Or use 2 tsp dried thyme.

ingredients.label: Carrot, diced (optional)
notes: Optional sweetness/body in gravy. Traditional versions may omit.

ingredients.label: Jamaican curry powder
notes: Choose a curry blend you like. Jamaican curry is often turmeric-forward.
If using very strong curry powder, start with 1.5 tbsp.

ingredients.label: Additional curry powder (to bloom in oil)
notes: Optional but recommended: blooming curry in oil deepens flavor and color.

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet pepper (whole or sliced)
notes: Keep whole for gentler heat; slice for hotter.
Habanero is a common substitute.

ingredients.label: Tomato paste
notes: Optional: helps deepen color and savory notes in the gravy.

ingredients.label: Stock (beef or chicken) or water
notes: Start with 4 cups; add more as needed to keep goat mostly submerged.
Water is fine; stock gives richer body.

ingredients.label: Potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
notes: Optional: classic in many curry goat pots. Add near the end to avoid mush.

ingredients.label: Coconut milk (optional)
notes: Not traditional in every Jamaican version; adds creaminess and softens heat.

steps.title: Clean and season the goat
instructions: Rinse goat pieces quickly under cool water if needed; drain well.
Toss goat with lime juice (or 1 tbsp vinegar), 1 tsp salt, black pepper, allspice, half the thyme, half the garlic, half the ginger, and half the scallions.
Cover and marinate at least 1 hour (best 4–12 hours) for deeper flavor.

steps.title: Brown the goat for depth
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy pot/Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Shake excess marinade from goat pieces (reserve marinade) and brown goat in batches 2–4 minutes per side until well colored.
Transfer browned goat to a plate; reduce heat to medium.
checks: Pot has browned bits (fond) but not burnt

steps.title: Bloom curry and build aromatics
instructions: Add diced onion (and carrot if using) to the pot; sauté 3–4 minutes until softened.
Stir in remaining garlic, ginger, and scallions; cook 30–60 seconds until fragrant.
Optional but recommended: add the extra 1 tbsp curry powder and stir 30–60 seconds to bloom in oil.
If using tomato paste, stir it in and cook 1 minute to darken slightly.
checks: Garlic is fragrant but not scorched

steps.title: Deglaze and braise until tender
instructions: Return browned goat (and any juices) to the pot. Add reserved marinade.
Add stock/water to mostly cover the goat. Add remaining thyme and the Scotch bonnet (whole for gentler heat).
Bring to a simmer, then cover and reduce heat to maintain a gentle simmer.
Braise 1 hour 30 minutes to 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until goat is tender and easily pulls from bone.
checks: Stew stays at a gentle simmer (not a hard boil)
Liquid level remains adequate; add splashes of water/stock as needed
Goat is tender before adding potatoes

steps.title: Add potatoes (optional) and finish gravy
instructions: If using potatoes, add them once goat is mostly tender (usually after ~90 minutes).
Simmer uncovered 20–30 minutes until potatoes are tender and gravy thickens.
If using coconut milk, stir in during the last 5 minutes (do not hard-boil after adding).
checks: Potatoes are tender but not falling apart

steps.title: Adjust seasoning and serve
instructions: Remove Scotch bonnet (if left whole) and thyme stems (if using sprigs).
Taste and adjust salt. If you want brighter finish, add a small squeeze of lime.
Serve hot with rice (white rice, rice & peas) and/or fried plantains.

refrigerator.guidance: Cool within 2 hours. Store in airtight container. Flavor improves overnight.

freezer.guidance: Freeze in portions with gravy. Thaw overnight in fridge; reheat gently.

best: Stovetop on low with a splash of water/stock
Covered in oven at 325°F until hot

batchNotes: Double batch is efficient: the same braise time yields more portions.
Freeze in 2-serving containers for quick future meals.
leftovers: Serve over rice or with flatbread
Shred and use for curry-goat tacos/wraps
Reduce gravy and serve as a thick stew

summary: Classic Caribbean curry goat: browned, then gently braised with Jamaican curry, thyme, aromatics, and optional Scotch bonnet until deeply tender in a rich gravy.

handling: Keep raw meat refrigerated; marinate in the fridge, not on the counter.
Avoid cross-contamination: sanitize board/knife after raw goat.
Bring stew to a full simmer when reheating; reheat until piping hot.

notes: Use Sauté to brown goat and bloom curry/aromatics as written.
Add liquid (start with 3 cups). Pressure cook High 35 minutes; natural release 15 minutes.
Simmer on Sauté to reduce gravy; add potatoes after pressure step and simmer until tender.
Skip Scotch bonnet or leave whole and remove early.
Increase thyme and scallion for aroma without heat.
Slice Scotch bonnet (gloves recommended) and add half during braise, half at the end.
Serve with pepper sauce.

variations.label: No potatoes (more traditional in some households)

notes: Skip potatoes and serve with rice & peas, yams, or dumplings instead.
Reduce uncovered 10–15 minutes for thicker gravy.
